page.title: How to get help
page.subtitle: If you need support right now, or know someone who does, here are resources available to you.

emergency.title: If there is immediate danger
emergency.intro: If you or someone nearby is in immediate danger, call emergency services.

resources.label: Emergency Services
resources.description: European emergency number, available in all EU countries.

mentalHealth.title: Mental health support
mentalHealth.intro: If you're going through a difficult time and need someone to talk to, these lines are available to listen.

primary.label: Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
primary.description: Call or text for confidential support during a mental health crisis.

primary.label: Samaritans
primary.description: Confidential emotional support for anyone in distress or despair.

others.label: Mental Health Crisis Line
others.description: Specialized crisis and suicide prevention support (in Spanish).

others.label: CVV — Centro de Valorização da Vida
others.description: Emotional support and suicide prevention (in Portuguese).

resources.label: National Domestic Violence Hotline
resources.description: Confidential support for domestic violence situations.

resources.label: Child Helpline
resources.description: Support for children and young people in difficulty.

resources.label: Mind
resources.description: Mental health information, advice and local support resources.

professional.title: Talk to a professional
professional.body: If it's not an emergency, but you feel like you need support, talking to a therapist or psychologist can make a real difference. You don't need to be in crisis to ask for help.
